method_atom: A technique for re-expressing a group-theoretic invariant amplitude — an invariant contraction of irreducible-representation labels attached to the elements of a combinatorial cell complex (a recoupling/'nj-symbol' coefficient) — in an overcomplete coherent-state basis of the underlying compact symmetry group, so that the basis parameters acquire a direct geometric (vector) interpretation. One then identifies a semiclassical/saddle-point regime in which a dominant subset of configurations controls the weighted sum, and imposes additional constraints that restrict a more general model to a target subsector, now written transparently in the new variables.
object_atom: The vertex amplitude of a spinfoam model of 4d quantum gravity, built from SU(2)/SO(4) BF topological field theory plus simplicity constraints, with the reinterpreted variables being the vectors normal to the triangles within each tetrahedron of the simplicial spacetime.
brief_reason: The coherent-state reparametrization of a representation-theoretic recoupling invariant, its saddle-point/semiclassical dominance, and the constraint-restriction step are the transferable machinery; the spinfoam BF-plus-constraints vertex on a simplicial 4-geometry is the quantum-gravity-specific object.
